Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

CTN 4, 094 [Šurpu 7][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[en₂] ⸢buru₅⸣ šag₄ abzu-ta [...]

[Incantation]. The sparrow [...] from within the Apsu.

⸢di⸣-me-tu₄ iš-tu qe₂-reb ap-[si-i ...]
o 2o 2

nam-⸢erim₂⸣ šag₄ an-na-⸢ta⸣ [...]

The oath [...] from within the sky.

ma-mi-tu₄ iš-⸢tu⸣ qe₂-reb AN⸢e⸣ [...]
o 3o 3

du₃-du₃ u₂-šim-gin₇ [...]

The ahhāzu-demon (Sum: dudu-demon) [...] like greenery.

aḫ-ḫa-zu ki-ma ur-qi₂-ti [...]
o 4o 4

tumu limmu₂-ba ⸢me⸣-lim₄ šu šuš₂-a-meš [...]

Towards the four cardinal points, they are overwhelmed by fearsome radiance like [...].

ana ša₂-a-ri er-bet-ti me-lam-me saḫ-pu ⸢ki⸣-[ma ...]
o 5o 5

uŋ₃ lu-a iri ⸢im⸣-ma-an-gig-ga-e-ne ⸢su⸣-[na ...]

They cause trouble to the people (Sum: numerous people) of the inhabited world; [...] their bodies.

UN-MEŠ da-ad₂-me ⸢u₂⸣-šam-ra-ṣu zu-⸢mur⸣-[ši-na ...]
o 6o 6

iri kalam-ma-ke₄ im-mi-in-še₈-⸢še₈⸣-e-⸢ne⸣ [...]

They cause city and land to wail; [...] the small one and the big one.

a-la u ma-a-tu u₂-šad-ma-mu ṣe-ḫer ⸢ra⸣-[bi ...]
o 7o 7

ŋuruš ki-sikil šu la₂-e-⸢ne⸣ [...]

They bind boy and girl by the hand; [...].

eṭ-lu u ar-⸢da-tu₄⸣ [...]
o 8o 8

an-ki-bi-ta ⸢muru₉-gin₇⸣ [...]

In heaven and on earth, they keep raining down like fine drizzle (Sum: like a rainstorm); [...].

ina ⸢ANe⸣ [u KI]⸢ti₃⸣ [ki-ma] ⸢im-ba-ri iz-za⸣-[an-nu-nu ...]
o 9o 9

[ki šag₄] ⸢dib⸣-ba diŋir-⸢re⸣-e-ne-ke₄ e-ne-⸢ne⸣-[ne ...]

They hurry to the place of the god's wrath and [...] silence.

⸢a-šar⸣ ki-mil-ti DINGIR šu₂-nu i-ḫi-iš-šu-ma qu-⸢u₂⸣-[la ...]
o 10o 10

lu₂ diŋir-bi ba-an-⸢da⸣-ri-meš gaba mu-un-⸢ri⸣ tug₂-⸢gin₇ ba-an⸣-dul-la-[meš]

They confronted the man whose god had abandoned him and covered him like a garment.

a-mi-lu ša₂ DINGIR-šu₂ ⸢it⸣-ti-<šu₂> is-su-<u> im-ḫu-ru-šu₂-⸢ma⸣ ki-⸢ma⸣ ṣu-ba-ti ik-⸢tu⸣-[mu-šu₂]
o 11o 11

[ugu]-⸢a-ni⸣ ba-an-si-eš uš₁₁-bi ⸢ŋal₂⸣-la-meš

They have charged upon him and filled him with poison.

[e-li]-⸢šu₂ i⸣-ši-ru-ma ⸢im-ta⸣ um-tal-lu-šu
o 12o 12

[...] ⸢la₂⸣-e-ne ⸢ŋiri₃-a-ni⸣ peš-peš-⸢e⸣-ne

They paralysed [...]; they swell his feet.

[...] ⸢uṣ⸣-ṣi-lu še-⸢pi⸣-šu ub-bi-ṭu
o 13o 13

[... im-mi]-⸢in⸣-diḫ-eš ze₂-ta ba-an-su₃-su₃

They infected [...] and have sprinkled bile on him.

[...] il-i-bu-ma mar-ta ⸢iz-za⸣-ar-qu-šu₂
o 14o 14

[... saŋ]-⸢ba⸣ adda-bi tab-tab-e-ne

[...] (and) oath give his body burning pains.

[... ma]-⸢mi⸣-tu pa-gar-šu u₂-ṣar-⸢ra⸣-pu
o 15o 15

[... BAR.ZI].⸢UD⸣ gaba-bi ⸢ḫu⸣-ḫu-luḫ-ḫa

[...] (and) phlegm have weakened his chest.

[...] i-rat-su u₂-tan-⸢niš⸣
o 16o 16

[...] ⸢si⸣-si₈-e-⸢ne⸣

[...] filled up (with) [...].

[...] um-tal-⸢li⸣
(rest of obverse missing)
Reverse
rr NaN  (start of reverse missing)
r 1'r 1'

[...] ⸢pi-qid-ma⸣

Entrust [...].

r 2'r 2'

[... kalag]-⸢ga-bi⸣ niŋ₂-⸢ki ki⸣-a u₃-me-ni-šum₂

May the god ditto (...), lord of the animals, present his severe illness to the small animals (Sum: caterpillars) of the ground.

[d]⸢MIN⸣<(...)> be-el nam-⸢maš⸣-ti ⸢mu-ru-us⸣-su dan-⸢nu zer⸣-man-di qaq-qa-ri li-šam-ḫir
r 3'r 3'

dda-mu šim-⸢mu₂ gal⸣ i₅-ŋar-bi ḫe₂-⸢en⸣-sag₁₀-⸢ga⸣

May Damu, the great exorcist, make his utterance acceptable.

dDA.⸢MU⸣ a-ši-pu ra-bu-u e-ger-ra-šu₂ li-⸢dam⸣-me-eq
r 4'r 4'

dnin-tin-ug₅-ga ⸢dama⸣ šu ḫal-bi šu-luḫ ⸢sikil⸣-bi ḫe₂-⸢em-ma-an-sed⸣-de₃

May the goddess who revives the dead, Gula, pacify him with the washing of her pure hands (Sum: May Nintinuga, the mother with cool hands, pacify him with her pure cleansing).

dbe-el-tu₄ ⸢mu⸣-bal-⸢li-ṭa⸣-at mi-i-ti dGU.LA ina me-si ŠU-MIN-ša₂ ⸢el-le-e-ti⸣ li-šap!(PA-)⸢šiḫ⸣-šu₂
r 5'r 5'

u₃ za-e d⸢asar-lu₂⸣-ḫi en šag₄-la₂-sug₄ ug₅-ga til₃-la ki ⸢aŋ₂-ŋa₂⸣

And you, Marduk (Sum: Asarluhi), merciful lord who loves to revive the dead,

u₃ at-ta dAMAR.UTU EN ⸢rem₂⸣-nu-u me₂-e-ta ⸢bul⸣-lu-ṭu i-ra-am-mu
r 6'r 6'

tu₆ nam-til₃-la-⸢zu⸣ na degₓ(RI)-ga kiŋ-gi₄-a u-me-ni-duḫ

undo his obligation with your pure incantation of life!

ina šip-ti-ka el-le-ti ša ba-la-ṭi ⸢eʾ⸣-il-ta-⸢šu₂⸣ pu-ṭur-ma
r 7'r 7'

lu₂-lu₇lu dumu diŋir-⸢ra⸣-na ḫe₂-en-kug-⸢ga⸣ ḫe₂-en-sikil-⸢la ḫe-en-dadag⸣-[ga]

May the man, the son of his god, become pure, clean and bright.

a-mi-lu mar DINGIR-šu₂ ⸢li⸣-lil li-bi-ib ⸢li⸣-[im]-⸢mir⸣
r 8'r 8'

dugbur šaŋan-gin₇ u-me-ni-ib₂-⸢luḫ⸣-[luḫ]

May he be cleansed like a flask on a base.

ki-ma BUR šiq-qa-⸢ti⸣ [...]
r 9'r 9'

[... i₃]-⸢nun-gin₇?⸣ [u-me-ni-su]-⸢ub⸣-[su-ub]

May he be rubbed like [...] for ghee.

⸢ki-ma⸣ [...]
r 10'r 10'

dutu ⸢saŋ⸣-[kal ...]

To Šamaš, foremost [...].

ana dša₂-maš [...]
r 11'r 11'

dutu saŋ-kal diŋir-⸢re⸣-[e-ne-ke₄ ...]

To Šamaš, foremost of the gods [...].

ana dša₂-maš a-ša₂-⸢red⸣ [...]
r 12'12'

EN₂ ni-iʾ-šu₂ [...]

Incantation. Difficulty in breathing, [...].
